title: Creating a table with a full outer join query gives type with wrong digits on the joined key.
poc: create table dummy4("key" varchar(32), val int);
create table dummy5("key" varchar(32), val int);
create table dummy6 as select "key", dummy4.val as "val4", dummy5.val as "val5" from dummy4 full outer join dummy5 using ("key");

select t.name as "table_name", c.name as "column_name", c.type, c.type_digits 
 from sys.tables t join sys.columns c on c.table_id = t.id where t.name = 'dummy6';
key_bug_features: Full outer join query on tables with mismatched key types
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table1 (id INT, name VARCHAR(50), value DECIMAL(10, 2));
CREATE TABLE test_table2 (id VARCHAR(10), description TEXT, amount FLOAT);
CREATE TABLE result_table AS SELECT t1.id, t1.name, t1.value, t2.description, t2.amount FROM test_table1 t1 FULL OUTER JOIN test_table2 t2 ON t1.id::VARCHAR = t2.id;
